entry.title: Re: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP &&&&
entry.content:
Hi @KristinaKunad 

Please can you clarify the following points.

  1. Will Walk Me have the functionality to create simulations as SAP Enable Now (SEN) does currecntly, hence replacing that element of SEN?
  2. Where you mention a transtion timeline/plan, will that also include how current SEN customers will migrate all of their simulations, quick reference guides etc over to the new Walk Me platform?

Thanks in advance

Scott

entry.title: Re: Saved SAP Systems in VS Code using SAP Fiori tools
entry.content:
Hello quiggs1000,
great instructions for configuration! I have the problem that when I click on test connection I get the following error:
This SAP system failed to return any services
Check the output tab for more details. You may need to increase the log level to Debug to see more details.

The following error appears in the debug console:

[error] The V2 request failed with error: Error parsing service list.

[error] The V4 request failed with error: Request failed with status code 403.

Have you encountered an error like this before?

entry.title: Need to Print Smartform on Zebra Bluetooth Printer ZQ320 from Mobile Computer TC58E
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

1. In SAP ECC 600 EHP7, SICF Service (for Custom Transaction having SMARTFORM for Label Print) Created and deployed on Zebra Mobile Computer TC58E.

2. Created Output Device, Device Type for Zebra Bluetooth Printer ZQ320.

How to send print from Zebra Mobile Computer TC58E to Zebra Bluetooth Printer ZQ320

Thanks.

Yogesh

entry.title: Re: Set up connection between SAP C4C (Sales Cloud) and SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @Schnobbo you can persist the remote tables too. I prefer the remote tables to not have an extra object once again (data flow). You will spare one step. Also if you do not use the first link, you will have limited tables to access

entry.title: Re: Agency profiles being purged due to non-acceptance of...
entry.content:
Hi @barbaragarcia ,

Did you find any workaround or solution for this?

I do have similar kind of requirement from client where recruitment process needs to be freezed unless dpcs is accepted by the agency candidate or manually added candidate by the recruiter in the talent pipeline.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Thank you for creating this space for the SAP Community! It's great to have a place where professionals can connect, exchange ideas, and engage in meaningful discussions.

Here, we look forward to insightful conversations, knowledge sharing, and networking with fellow SAP experts. In return, we aim to contribute by actively participating, sharing experiences, and fostering a collaborative environment.

Looking forward to connecting and learning from each other!

entry.title: C_MATERIALSTOCKACTUAL can not be opened in "Custom Querys". Error states CDS entity cannot be used.
entry.content:
Within SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud C_MATERIALSTOCKACTUAL cannot be opened.

Why? It is released and all other Query can be opened. I can rebuild it since I know the Cube, but it should work, otherwise I dont need standard queries

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to include the attachments for invoice...
entry.content:
Hello Angeline,

My team is responsible for the Manage Recurring Supplier Invoices app. After consulting team experts, it's confirmed that the attachments of a recurring supplier invoice will NOT be copied to individual invoices when they are posted periodically.

Kind regards, Anne

entry.title: Critical Success Factors for a Seamless SAP S/4HANA Transition
entry.content:
For many companies, the move to SAP S/4HANA is a strategic step toward digital transformation. The benefits are clear—real-time analytics, streamlined processes, and a modern user interface. However, the transition often turns out to be more complex than initially expected.

From my experience with SAP S/4HANA projects, these are some of the most critical factors for success:

  1. Choosing the Right Migration Approach

The first major decision is the migration strategy. There is no one-size-fits-all approach, but selecting the right path early on is crucial:

  • Greenfield: A complete system redesign, ideal for companies looking to optimize processes but requiring significant change management.
  • Brownfield: A system conversion that retains existing structures, making the transition smoother but limiting flexibility.
  • Selective Data Transition: A hybrid approach that allows companies to retain critical historical data while restructuring processes where needed.

Many companies struggle with this choice. What has worked best in your experience?

  1. Process Harmonization and the Role of SAP Standard

An SAP S/4HANA project is more than just a technical migration—it is an opportunity to rethink processes. A key question in every project is:

  • How much standardization is feasible?
  • Which custom developments are truly necessary?

The closer companies stay to the SAP standard, the easier future upgrades become, and the lower the long-term maintenance effort. However, business-specific requirements must also be carefully considered.

  1. Data Migration – A Critical Success Factor

Data quality often becomes a major roadblock in SAP S/4HANA projects. Missing, duplicate, or inconsistent data can lead to significant delays and operational disruptions. A well-defined data cleansing strategy and thorough test migrations are essential to ensure a smooth go-live.

  1. Change Management and Training

A successful transition to SAP S/4HANA is not just about technology—it’s also about people. Many companies underestimate the impact of change on end users. The most successful projects focus on:

  • Early communication about what is changing and why
  • Comprehensive training programs for key users and end users
  • Post-go-live support to address issues quickly and ensure adoption

Without a structured change management approach, even the most technically sound implementation can face resistance from users.

  1. Leveraging SAP BTP and Automation

SAP S/4HANA provides a solid foundation, but many companies look beyond the core system to drive additional value. The SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) enables:

  • Fiori applications for an improved user experience
  • AI-driven automation to reduce manual effort
  • Cloud and hybrid integration through the SAP Integration Suite

The challenge is to determine which innovations truly add value without adding unnecessary complexity.

Final Thoughts

Moving to SAP S/4HANA is not just an IT project—it is a business transformation. Companies that carefully evaluate their migration strategy, focus on data quality, embrace standardization where possible, and invest in change management are the ones that achieve long-term success.

What have been your biggest challenges in SAP S/4HANA projects? How did you approach them? I’d love to hear your insights.

entry.title: Offer Letter Conditional Tokens
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

We are implementing a conditional token to our offer letters.

We have two languages in the system (en_US and de_DE) and the same conditional token should resolve to different parts of text based on the language of the offer letter template and the value of a picklist field from offer approval.

However, when we trigger the offer letter the token does not resolve. We have created a rule trace and seems that the issue is here: 'Compare 'null' with 'en_US' by operator 'EQUAL'. Result: 'false'.' The language of offer letter is considered 'null' and, as a result, the condition is false.  Here is a screenshot from system when the user selects the offer letter:

If we delete the condition for the language and just keep the picklist condition, then the token resolves but we will have the same text for en_US and de_DE.

Have you ever implemented conditional tokens that should resolve to different parts of text based on offer letter language and can share a solution?

Any suggestions are highly appreciated!

Many thanks in advance,

Leandra

entry.title: When I move to Quality system from Dev my BW Query OData service, it is moved "proforma".
entry.content:
Hello to all of you. The problem is as follows:

I develop OData service with all the artifacts based on Query. The tests are ok, and when I move to Quality system I see that in the service persists in /iwfnd/maint_service but the REG_SERVICE and REG_MODEL tables doesnt have the corresponding records. The service and its belonging model are missing.
For example the Model (XXXX_MDL) is available in table /IWBEP/I_MGW_OHT in the development system, but when moved it is missing in quality. When I execute the service in /iwfnd/gw_client, I got status code Error 500 - No service found for namespace /SAP/.....

entry.title: Re: I am trying to send auto email to vendor when goods r...
entry.content: @Raymond sir, we are in SAP HANA Public cloud, here ABAPer codification not there

entry.title: Eligibility to use SAP University Alliances
entry.content: Please bear in mind that our SAP University Alliances program would only be eligible for your degree programs, not professional learning courses.

entry.title: Re: MISSING MANDATE REFERENCE
entry.content: Hello Henrike, no I'm sorry, but this is not our case, we are asking why journal entries created in finance are missing of the "mandate reference", nothing related to prenotification. Regards Davide

entry.title: Re: Installation of equipment to functional location via...
entry.content:
Hi adrakev,

The simple answer is no - it is not possible.

The way of doing it is as follows:
1. You create a Corrective Maintenance order, release it.
2. Technician starts the physical work, de-installs/installs new equipment etc.
3. Technician performs time confirmations and spare parts GI (if relevant) against this maint. order in SAP.
4. Technician/Maintenance Supervisor de-installs/installs new equipment in SAP via IE4N. As a reference, the corrective maintenance order from point 1. is used. 

Alternatively - if your case does not require inventory movements, equipment can be 'deinstalled/installed' in master data. You go to the equipment, structure tab and install it there under specific FLOC or superior equipment. However, if your assets are also stock managed, then IE4N is the only way of doing this.

Best,
Dan

entry.title: Re: Client Handling and Client Isolation in ABAP
entry.content:
I have made it client safe, but when I tried to release stability contract(c1)/stability contract(c4)  I have got the attached error. AMDP get_data is not cds session client dependent or client in dependent.

My CDS Table Function:

@EndUserText.label: 'Table Function for IPD PACK SERV'

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@ClientHandling.clientSafe:true

@ClientHandling.algorithm: #SESSION_VARIABLE

@ClientHandling.type: #CLIENT_DEPENDENT

define table function ZP_TF_IPD_PACK_SERVICE

returns {

client : abap.clnt;

patientid : zpatient_id;

 

}

implemented by method ZCL_TF_IPD_PACK_SERVICE=>get_data;

 

My AMDP Table Function Class:

CLASS zcl_tf_ipd_pack_service DEFINITION

PUBLIC

FINAL

CREATE PUBLIC .

 

PUBLIC SECTION.

INTERFACES if_amdp_marker_hdb .

CLASS-METHODS: get_data FOR TABLE FUNCTION zp_tf_ipd_pack_service.

PROTECTED SECTION.

PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.

 

 

 

CLASS zcl_tf_ipd_pack_service IMPLEMENTATION.

METHOD get_data BY DATABASE FUNCTION FOR HDB

LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT

OPTIONS READ-ONLY

USING zpatient_reg.

RETURN select bed.client as Client, bed.zpatient_id as Patientid from zpatient_reg as bed

where bed.client = session_context('CLIENT')

order by bed.zpatient_id ;

endmethod.

 

ENDCLASS.

 

can anyone help me on this...

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2502), SAP BTP, ABAP environment

entry.title: Re: Mass change of equipment
entry.content: Is there any app or LTMC template to achieve mass update of technical objects

entry.title: Re: Error "can not copy the lexicon" during installation
entry.content: I have also got the same problem. But for my case, Package "chkconfig-1.20-2.el9.x86_64" is missing on the RHEL server. After installing this package issue has been resolved.

entry.title: Experience SAP Solutions in Action - Navigating the Energy Transition at EUC 2025
entry.content:
Dear Energy and Utilities Community,

The time has come once again! This year, we are delighted to invite you to the SAP Energy and Utilities Conference (EUC) in the dynamic city of Rotterdam, from April 8th to 10th.

The conference will feature a lineup of talks from esteemed customers, valued partners, and inspiring thought leaders. At our SAP booth in the exhibition, attendees will have the opportunity to gain valuable insights into the topics shaping our industries today.

Building on the increased interest we've seen in our demo booth over the past three years, we're delighted to offer a wide array of solution demos once again at the SAP booth.

Our SAP industry experts will be on hand to showcase these solutions, answer your questions, provide personalized advice, and offer deep insights into the innovative solutions SAP brings to the table.

Curious about what awaits you at our booth? You can book the relevant demos in the Event App which will be launched around mid-March.

 

 

SAP Booth @ EUC 2024 – Seville, Spain.

Utilities Demos:

Asset Management & Supply Chain

  • RISE with SAP for Utilities – Enterprise Asset Management: The demo focuses on the operation and maintenance phase of the asset life cycle. Utilities have thousands of assets in the field and in the plants, and the performance of these assets is a critical part of their business. Technology can be used to monitor these assets and predict problems before they occur or before they become critical. The demo shows how an error in a transformer is detected early enough based on sensor data and then repaired by the field crew before an outage occurs. This includes the analysis of the sensor data, the triggering and planning of a work order, the purchase of required spare parts, scheduling of work, the actual work on the field, and analyzing the efforts. 

 

  • Smart Power Plant Demo: Experience live the insights of the SAP Smart Power Plant from the SAP Walldorf campus.

 

  • Geo Framework with SAP Enterprise Product Development Visualization of a Transformer Station: Users can leverage map-driven technology to streamline decision-making by seamlessly integrating business assets, such as equipment and maintenance orders, with geographic information systems (GIS). Through the Geographical Enablement Framework, companies can enhance efficiency and connectivity by enriching SAP Business Suite and SAP S/4HANA solutions with spatial awareness features, bridging the gap between GIS and business applications. This integration facilitates smoother workflows, empowering users to drive business processes directly from the map interface, thus achieving true data federation and optimizing decision-making capabilities.

 

  • Intelligent Asset Management e2e demo: a demo that walks through end-to-end process for Intelligent Asset Management solutions (SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management, SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration, SAP Asset Performance Management, SAP S/4HANA Asset Management for Resource Scheduling, SAP Field Service Management, SAP Service and Asset Manager).

 

  • Scheduling / Mobile Service and Asset Management: SAP Service and Asset Manager enables maintenance technicians to view their assigned work orders and perform work order confirmations on a mobile device (iPad). Maintenance technicians can also create new work orders and/or notifications on their own to report additional findings and trigger follow-up processes. In this case all relevant steps will be performed on the mobile device.

 

  • From Request to Resolution: Faster Meter Swaps with SAP Field Service Management: Efficient meter installations and exchanges require seamless coordination between customers, technicians, and back-office teams. SAP Field Service Management optimizes the process with AI-driven scheduling, mobile execution, and automated compliance documentation—enhancing efficiency, reducing delays, and improving customer satisfaction.

 

Customer Experience

  • SAP Service Cloud Version 2: Numerous trends are pushing utilities companies to adopt more customer-centric approaches and provide seamless and personalized service. Experience the SAP Service Cloud Utilities add-on to learn how you can orchestrate utilities processes directly from SAP Service Cloud​. Watch the video.

 

  • Customer Service processes in the Customer Engagement: The interaction center agents can use the interaction center as a central point of entry to search and manage the Utilities master data as well as to carry out the traditional Utilities industry processes such as meter reading, bill correction, move-in/out and more. This demo is a walkthrough of features and functions available with this solution to help Utilities CSR to perform his / her job effectively.

 

  • SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement – Marketing Automation for Utilities: Accelerate time to value, meet customers where they are with what they want, and produce measurable results with real-time, scalable personalization.

 

Energy & Markets

  • SAP Cloud for Energy: Get to know SAP Cloud for Energy, the platform for the complete metering data lifecycle management. Check out these videos Operational Monitoring, Solve Issues, Device Master Data, Configuration.

 

  • Managing the increasing complexity of metered installations in a world of Distributed Energy Resources with the Measurement Concept Management: SAP Cloud for Utilities foundation, also known as Measurement Concept Management, or MCM for short, is a new SAP solution aimed mainly at the distributed generation market. The solution serves to enable customers to manage, streamline and unify processes around installations including decentralized energy resources throughout their whole lifecycle, from their creation to the final shutdown. Read more about SAP Cloud for Utilities foundation here:

 

  • SAP Energy Park: SAP executing on the Energy Transition: The SAP Energy Park initiative aims to not only transform or own operations but also empower other organizations to embrace the energy transition through a combination of cutting-edge technology, innovative real-life scenarios, and strategic partnerships.

 

  • BeFlexible: European Flexibility Management Platform: BeFlexible is the innovation project for the Digital Network and Platform for power grids within the European CEO Alliance. It aims at increasing the flexibility of the energy system, improving the cooperation between DSOs TSOs, facilitating the participation of all energy-related stakeholders, enabling to build non-energy services.

 

  • From Quote to Install – Streamlining Solar PV Projects with SAP Field Service Management: Utilities are expanding into renewable energy services, including photovoltaic (PV) system installations. SAP Field Service Management simplifies the end-to-end process—from scheduling site assessments for quote generation to coordinating multi-stage installations and managing external contractors. With AI-driven planning, mobile tools for technicians, and seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA, utilities can efficiently deliver solar projects while ensuring a smooth customer experience.

 

  • E-Mobility - Billing & operations of EV charging services: 
    • EV charging point management with SAP E-Mobility.
    • EV fleet charging management and EV charging solutions for condominiums
    • Efficient and integrated billing of EV charging cards and charging solution offerings with SAP Subscription Billing and S/4HANA.
    • Billing & operations of EV charging services with SAP: Build, run, and manage electric vehicle charging networks with integration into end-to-end billing processes on one platform. Get more details.

 

SAP S/4HANA Utilities / SAP Utilities Core

  • SAP Utilities Core: Meter to Cash and New Connection Process: SAP Utilities Core is our solution offering addressing the core Utilities industry meter-to-cash process including customer service and market process management. Read more here 

 

  • Machine Learning for implausible meter readings and outsorted billing documents: The demo showcases the specific capabilities of machine learning-based processing for implausible meter readings. It is seamlessly integrated in meter reading processing in IS-U, learns from the meter reading and consumption history, and provides the capability to determine a machine learning release confidence used to automate the release of implausible meter readings.

 

  • Automatic Implausible MR Correction using Business Rules (BRFplus): One of the key processes for a Utility is the meter to cash process, which starts from the reading of the meter, billing and invoicing, and collecting the payment. At each step of the process, there are validation rules put in place to ensure the correct data is captured, billed and collected from the customer. The Demo focuses on the first step of the process capturing discrete meter reads from external systems and performing validations. This function is part of the SAP S/4HANA Utilities solution and is executed by Device Management.

 

  • SAP FIORI Apps Billing and Meter Reading: See the new user experience for dedicated metering and billing roles and the possibilities to branch from KPI-bar charts through list views into a detailed single customer case.

 

  • Business Process Exception Management - KPI Designer: The focus of this Demo will be utilizing the Business Process Exception Management (BPEM) data to illustrate how the application can improve the management of exceptions. Charts and tables will be created to view data. Semantic objects will be defined to provide the ability to execute business processes and/or transactions against the BPEM data without logging into another application.

 

  • How to boost your extra commodity business with an end-2-end order to cash: In the digital economy, the order-to-cash process is a critical driver of business success, especially for companies offering subscription-based products. Addressing the complexities of this process requires a sophisticated approach to manage product lifecycle, billing, and financial operations efficiently. Our Order-to-Cash Solution, l is crafted to optimize the entire lifecycle of subscription products. This transformative solution encompasses comprehensive billing, invoice consolidation, and financial management, underpinned by advanced features such as complex calculation models and wallet concepts.

 

  • Empower and optimizing your bundling and consolidation for your extra commodity business: In response to the growing demand for sustainable energy solutions and the seamless integration of renewable technology into consumers' lifestyles, our company is excited to present a groundbreaking business initiative focused on extra commodities such as Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging and/or Solar Panels scenarios through a subscription billing model. This innovative approach aims to eliminate the financial and logistical barriers traditionally associated with acquiring and maintaining such technologies, while promoting eco-friendly, energy-efficient living.

 

Water

  • Smart Water Platform for Remote Water Quality Monitoring and Leakage Detection: Get insights on this platform watching this video

 

  • SAP Utilities Core for Water: Meter to cash for Water platform in SAP S/4HANA: Reduce architecture complexity and gain operational efficiency moving into a single. Get some insights watching this video

 

Utilities Analytics

  • S.MART Energy Shop: Journey from Energy Consumer to Prosumer: SAP and partners solutions provide the right capabilities for Utilities to guide their customers to become and active member of the energy transition, leading to a more decentralized, digitalized and sustainable energy system.

 

  • Analytics, Billing and Meter Reading: Learn how to easily configure your own individualized report- based standard analytical scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Utilities.

 

  • SAP S/4HANA Utilities Content for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere

 

Oil, Gas & Energy Demos:

  • Shutdown Turnaround & Outage Solution for Refineries by Wipro: This solution jointly developed with WIPRO as our partner where you will see comprehensive and integrated STO management solution. Which addresses customer challenges as diverse as end-to-end STO processes, scope management, non-integrated planning, long lead items tracking, safety, scheduling, real-time effective reporting, control of costs, risks, procurement, and user-friendliness.

 

  • SAP Field Logistics Management - Efficiently manage Upstream remote logistics: In this demo, customers can see end to end process of how the SAP Field Logistics solution helps to manage the movement of products required for maintenance operations between a supplying base and a remote location.

 

  • Follow the molecule throughout the commodity value chain using SAP Solution CTRM & Hydrocarbon supply chain Solution: End-to-end visibility of the commodity value chain delivering natively integrated capabilities incorporating Trading, Risk Management, Scheduling and Settlement with focus on margin and inventory precision.
  • Further demos we are going to show - stayed tuned for a detailed information:
    • Predictive Maintenance Demo
    • Digital Twins driving the future of Business
    • SAP Prepayment Solution
    • Secondary Distribution for Oil, Gas, and Energy
    • Retail Fuel Network Operations
    • Joint Venture Accounting - Joint Interest Billing
    • Primary Distribution for Oil, Gas, & Energy

Cross-Industry Demos:

  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower - Holistic Steering and Reporting: Experience a comprehensive approach to sustainability management with our SAP Sustainability Control Tower demo. This interactive showcase will illustrate how organizations can effectively steer, track, and report their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) initiatives.

 

  • SAP Green Token: Stakeholders want to know the origin and sustainability of their products. SAP Green Token supports material traceability and transparency with segregation or mass balancing, tokenization, and a blockchain-based chain of custody for trust and compliance with recognized standards.

 

  • SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, and Quote) for B2C and B2B Offers and Quotations: See how SAP CPQ handles complex configuration of products and comprehensive quotation processes.

 

Business AI for Energy and Utilities:

  • Increasing User Productivity with SAP Joule: We are excited to introduce you to Joule, your new virtual bestie, embedded in SAP Solutions. Joule makes every task more interactive, fun and - most importantly - makes SAP Solutions even easier to use. Explore now, how Joule can help to increase user productivity within SAP Solutions.

 

  • Create Job Descriptions using SAP Joule: Explore now, how Joule embedded in SAP SuccessFactors can support recruiters and hiring managers to create job description effectively, to make a hiring process more efficient and to find the right candidate in a short period of time!

 

  • Intelligent CX with SAP CX AI Toolkit - Proactive and Textual Intelligence: The CX AI Toolkit provides several users with insights and Gen-AI use cases that boost productivity across Sales, Service, and Commerce. On top of that it is highly flexible to be designed around your specific business requirements. Visit us for a demo and see the solution in action.

 

  • How to improve your customer financial management with new and improved AI capabilities: In the era of digital transformation, organizations are increasingly turning to artificial intelligence (AI) to optimize and enhance financial operations. Our latest solution focuses on revolutionizing the contract account receivable and payable processes through the integration of AI-driven features such as behavior insights, cash application, and dispute management. This demo showcases how these technologies can streamline financial workflows, improve cash flow, and enhance decision-making, ultimately contributing to better financial health and operational efficiency.

 

  • Business AI use cases and accelerators for Oil, Gas and Energy:
    • Abnormal behavior prediction in SAP Analytics
    • Zero touch for PRA
    • Digital Assistant for Ownership Portal

 

  • GenAI-based Digital Agent for Customers Self-Service for Utilities: A conversation-driven self-service agent for utility customers that offers an alternative to traditional self-service portals. It delivers a complete experience through an interactive, natural language interface with a chat-like design, enhancing user engagement and efficiency.
  • Further planned demos - stay tuned for more details:
    • Resolving implausible meter readings automatically with AI in SAP S/4HANA Utilities
    • SAP Market Communication for Utilities, AI-assisted CONTRL-message explanation
    • GenAI-enabled Commodity Deal Capture
    • Where is my stuff

 

SAP Signavio:

  • Leverage SAP's Toolchain and Utilities content to accelerate your transformation: Get a hands-on view on how SAP's powerful toolchain, combined with industry-specific content, accelerates transformation projects for utilities. We'll dive into process analysis and modeling with SAP Signavio, landscape management with LeanIX, and implementation best practices using SAP CALM.

"In case you haven't registered for our event yet, please register here.

 

 

 

We are looking forward to meeting you in Rotterdam!

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud API for reading customer open item data
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues, 

Im trying to find the correct API for reading customer open item data. 
The use case is as follows:
An external system is sending custom open item data to the SAP public cloud system and SAP PC is managing the open items. The external system needs to get the status of the customer open items from SAP. I assume a standard read API is available in SAP public cloud, but I do not want to use FI-CA if possibe and I cannot find the correct API to use. 

The following APIs do not seem to be availabe for public cloud?

Customer Invoice - Read | SAP Help Portal

Customer Open Item | SAP Help Portal

Please help me find the correct documentation / API to read customer open item data in SAP public cloud, that can be triggerd from an external system.

KR
Andreas Winter

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS3: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?
entry.content:
All good things come in threes – therefore please find below (continuing my series of my previous SAP Community blog posts listed at the bottom) an overview of the main innovations dedicated to the utilities industry that are new with SAP S/4HANA FPS3 2023, the third feature pack available for SAP S/4HANA release 2023 since end of February 2025:

  1. Once more the latest feature pack of SAP S/4HANA Utilities offers further modern and flexible analytical capabilities: In continuation of previously delivered roadmap items, you can now extract energy data management (EDM) master data for reporting in SAP Datasphere such as profile headers and profile assignments. For that purpose new CDS views are  available, for details see Energy data management master data for reporting in SAP Datasphere for the utilities industry.
    The second delivered road map item in the area of analytics is Native sales statistics and unbilled revenue reporting in SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud. Existing processes and capabilities, such as statistics groups and their assignments, ODP data sources, ODP delta queue, simulation period, and IS-U mass simulation index are being reused. For more technical details please read SAP note 3473213.

  2. Several enhancements are available related to the topics Sales Interface Integration and Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL), and mainly targeting retailers in liberalized energy markets:
    • Period control and APIs for life cycle events and contract management processes allows you to define and calculate durations and contract periods using product attributes. These appointments are shown in the utilities sales contract overview as part of S/4HANA Utilities for customer engagement

      Moreover new OData APIs are available to read utilities sales contracts and simulate pricing for utilities. For details see APIs for Utilities Products, Utilities Sales Contracts and Utilities Sales Contract Processes.

    • Streamlining contract migration and bulk data processing offers the ability to migrate relevant parts of the contract history to utilities sales contracts. This history is being shown in utilities sales contract overview, where you can also cancel selected utilities sales contract items in case this is needed after migration.
    • In the context of the Utilities Product Integration Layer (UPIL) and the Sales Integration Interface, it is now possible to use for instance a characteristic for a product-determined price amount to a sales product for a quantity-based price. You can maintain prices, amounts etc. directly on product level. For all details see Extending pricing capabilities.

  3. Once more we have also incorporated enhancements to SAP S/4HANA Utilities based on improvement requests submitted by utility companies from various countries within our SAP Continuous Influence session dedicated to SAP S/4HANA Utilities. For all details see my blog post SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities: Status Update January 2025  .

  4. Following the developer extensibility element of SAP's Clean Core strategy, several additional cloud compliant business add-ins (BAdIs) are now available for the most used SAP enhancements (aka user-exits) in IS-U. Please read SAP note 3406389 for a comprehensive overview on Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Utilities, review the list of related SAP notes.
     
  5. Finally the SAP Fiori app  Business Partner Financial Overview now includes a new facet "IS-U Invoicing Documents" showing utilities-industry specific data



    For details see Display of utilities invoices and budget billing plans in a business partner financial overview .

  6. During the last months, the central SAP for Utilities landing page on SAP Help Portal has been restructured and updated in order to provide a comprehensive overview about SAP S/4HANA Utilities and other available SAP solutions dedicated to the utilities industry. Follow https://help.sap.com/utilities to explore all details.

Finally, as a reference, here ist the list of my previous blog posts related to SAP S/4HANA 2023:

SAP S/4HANA 2023: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry? 
SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry? 
SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2: What’s in it for the Utilities Industry?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hello !Happy to connect with all of you, this is Marian from Romania. Looking forward to be part of this wonderful community and learn!

entry.title: Re: I am trying to send auto email to vendor when goods r...
entry.content: You should add the correct tag to the question - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Andreas 

First of all thanks a lot for this blog to get us all started on the AI / ML journey! I tried to follow the steps you outlined as is, and I have the following questions, hope you can enlighten.

1) When I include massive=True parameter in AdditiveModelForecast, it gives me error that says 'feature not supported: Cannot use local temporary table'  leaving it blank as in AdditiveModelForecast(), then there is no error. What is the significance of this massive=True, and is it functionally ok to execute this method without this parameter?

2) After my AdditiveModelForecast() call, I call the fit() command with group_key, as in:

amf = AdditiveModelForecast()
amf.fit(data=df_rem_agg, group_key='MATNR') 
 
But actually, my data model looks like this:
MATNR,CATEGORY,ERDAT,QTY
000000000013204583,ASORT1,20241004,0.250000000000000
000000000013204538,ASORT2,20240829,0.400000000000000
000000000013204538,ASORT2,20240829,0.400000000000000
000000000013204538,ASORT2,20240829,0.400000000000000
000000000013204537,ASORT3,20240829,0.222000000000000
000000000013204537,ASORT3,20240829,0.300000000000000
 
Means I want to group by MATNR and CATEGORY for the time series model, and not just MATNR, but the group_key only accepts string, not array. I thought I can enter it as group_key=['MATNR','CATEGORY']
 
Is this a limitation, or there is another way of defining this based on two dimensions(MATNR and CATEGORY)?
 
3) The example is also using read_csv for the input data. I have extracted the CSV but my real data model is in my HANA Database actually, not as a single table, but a result of multiple joins. I'm aware that I can refer to HANA table using 
conn.table('Table_name', schema='schema_name'), but it does not allow a whole SELECT statement in replacement of the Table_name.
Is this a limitation, or there is another way to refer to a HANA source that is defined by joins?
 
4) There is a reference to df_rem_countries but I don't see it defined anywhere, so I'm kind of stuck.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to change target mapping parameters for a fiori app?
entry.content:
Hi,

If you need to change parameters in a SAP-standard target mapping it will break the reference to the original catalog.

My suggestion is to make your own copy of the SAP-standard target mapping to your own technical catalog.

There you can change the parameters as you see fit and then reference this target mapping from your business catalog.

Hopefully that helps.

Nils

entry.title: BI Launchpad Log out - Leave Site message
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

it appears that since BI4.3 SP04 Patch 9 that when a user logs out of BI Launchpad, the user now has to respond to a non Business Objects generated dialog box

Leave site?

Changes you made may not be saved.

The entire browser remains unresponsive until the user responds to the dialog

(also attached as a picture)

I cannot believe that a non Business Objects dialog box like this can be deemed to part of the standard Business Objects workflow.

Has anyone else encountered this and more importantly, has anyone come up with a workaround?

 

regards

 

James

entry.title: Using SNC on IBM i SAP NetWeaver RFC SDK 7.50
entry.content:
Hello,

to secure our SAP NetWeaver RFC SDK net communication, we want to use SNC. For that we must download the CommonCryptoLib.

On Windows we can use the environment variable SNC_LIB and set it to to that path of sapcrypto.dll.

On IBM i we use the ILE version (LIBSAPNRFC.SRVPGM), but on the SAP Download site of CommonCryptoLib there is only a PASE version available. Is the ILE version of LIBSAPNRFC is able to use the PASE version of the CommonCryptoLib? Or is there also a ILE version (*SRVPGM) available?

Best regards,
Matthias Schatte

entry.title: SAP screens not detected as SAP GUI while capturing an Application
entry.content:
Hello Bot Devs,
In my SAP IRPA Cloud studio project, I am facing a problem with SAP GUI screen capturing, where both client and server side scripting is enabled. still while capturing a SAP screen, the tool only identifies it through UI automation and not SAP GUI technology.
My colleague who is using the same settings, system as well as the same project is able to do the same and capture the screens with SAP GUI technology without any problem.

Colleague screen

my screen


We are using the same version, build and patch level of SAP Logon application.

At this point I am not really sure what I am missing here.

SAP Desktop Agent Version 3.38.59


@peterengel @SAPSupport 
It would be great if you could help us here to identify the problem.

entry.title: unable to install sap cloud business application tools in eclipse 2024-12
entry.content:
unable to install from web site

https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/2019-12

sap cloud business application tools.

 

I am using eclipse 2024-12 edition.

entry.title: Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter
entry.content:
Continuing my SAP CI Pagination blog series, this time with Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter!

When integrating with Microsoft Dynamics CRM, handling large datasets efficiently is key to ensuring smooth data synchronization. Unlike Salesforce, which relies on SOQL and nextRecordsUrl, Dynamics CRM follows an OData-based pagination approach using <odata.nextLink> to fetch records in manageable batches.

In this blog, I’ll cover:
1. How pagination works in the Microsoft Dynamics CRM adapter
2. Best practices to optimize API calls and avoid performance bottlenecks
3.A step-by-step approach to implementing custom pagination in SAP CI

If you missed the first part on Salesforce Adapter, check it out here Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Salesforce Adapter 

Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter - Handling Large Data Sets with Pagination

In this use case, we focus on retrieving records from the accounts object in Microsoft Dynamics CRM, which contains 10K+ records. To ensure efficient data retrieval, we utilize Advanced OData Queries along with the pagination feature in the receiver adapter.

Fetching data page by page, processing each batch, and then moving to the next page is the best approach for handling large datasets. In this blog, I will explain the end-to-end batching process, ensuring seamless data synchronization while optimizing API performance.

Design Approach: 

The scenario involves sending accounts object data from Microsoft Dynamics CRM to a target system that has a batch limit of 500 records per request. To accommodate this constraint, we implement a pagination approach using two Microsoft Dynamics CRM receiver adapter call.

The call fetches the page of records within a looping process to retrieve the pages one after the other. Each fetched page is passed through message mapping, ensuring seamless transformation before being delivered to the target system. This cycle continues—fetching a page, processing it, and sending it to the target—until all records are transferred, ensuring an efficient full end to end Batching processing and controlled data flow from Microsoft Dynamics CRM to the target system.

 

Design components elaboration:

Timer : This is a timer based flow which be a scheduled as per the user's input

Content Modifier : "set properties" - This step is used to accept inputs such as ObjectName, fieldnames, StartDate from the user, all these fields are externalized to make it dynamic. 

Looping Process Call : "Initiate MS Dynamics Call" -  This Looping Process Call continuously runs until the <odata.nextLink> field is absent from the response payload. The condition for looping is:

${property.NextPageLink} != ''

Here’s how it works:
-- The NextPageLink property is extracted from the response payload.
-- If NextPageLink exists, it is used in the next request to fetch the next set of records.
-- The loop continues until <odata.nextLink> is no longer present, indicating the last page.

This ensures efficient batch processing, fetching all records page by page while maintaining seamless data flow.

The Microsoft Dynamics CRM receiver adapter is placed inside the Looping Process Call to fetch records page by page.

Processing Tab

-- Pagination is enabled, ensuring controlled data retrieval.
-- Page Size is set to "500", based on the specific requirement.
-- The Next Page Link option is assigned the value "${property.NextPageLink}", which dynamically comes from the response payload.

We are not digging into the connection tab in this blog, this blog focuses only on pagination concept.

Output:

Page 1 :  Response from the Microsoft Dynamics CRM system contains 500 records and "odata.nextLink" which has the URL to the next page.

Page 2: Response from the Microsoft Dynamics CRM system contains 500 records and "odata.nextLink" which has the URL to the next page.

Content Modifier : "Read nextURL" - This is a crucial step where are we reading key field —<odata.nextLink> using xpath.

Process Call : "Message Mapping" - 

After fetching each page, the data is passed to Message Mapping, where it is processed before being sent to the target system. This step is target-specific and is not covered in this blog.

For demonstration purposes, I have included this step to mimic the "send to target" operation after message mapping. The key focus of this blog remains on pagination handling in the Microsoft Dynamics CRM adapter and ensuring seamless data retrieval.

 

Final Page Handling: Completing the Pagination Process

For a total of 10573 records, the pagination process spans 22 pages:

  • First 21 pages contain 500 records each.
  • Page 22 contains the remaining 73 records.

When the 22th page is fetched, the response contains:

  • No <odata.nextLink> , confirming that no more pages are available.

At this point, the looping process terminates, ensuring that all records have been successfully retrieved and processed.

Page 22

From the TRACE logs below, its evident that looping process call "Initiate MS Dynamics Call" executed 23 times indicating 23 pages.

Full Run in TRACE

 

Conclusion

Implementing pagination in SAP CI for the Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter ensures efficient data retrieval while preventing performance bottlenecks. By leveraging looping mechanisms, we can seamlessly fetch and process large datasets in manageable batches. This approach not only optimizes API calls but also ensures smooth end-to-end data transfer. Stay tuned for the next part of this series, where we explore pagination for next adapters.

I hope this helps.

Cheers,

Punith Oswal

entry.title: Re: ABAP REDUCE with SORT
entry.content: it's inline declaration, as in SELECT * FROM TAB INTO TABLE DATA I_TAB

entry.title: Re: Bug 2fa Sync to SAP Commerce Cloud Portal login not u...
entry.content: You can try to create a service request in order to reset 2FA. See KBA 3387869 for reference (check CX Commerce - Disable Two-Factor Authentication). Hopefully it will resolve your issue.

entry.title: Re: ABAP REDUCE with SORT
entry.content: thanks. then I guess, better explicitly declared internal table rather than inline to not have unexpected behavior

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Populating Dynamic Default Values in Analytical Model Variables
entry.content:
Hello, @Vajrakanth_Siddu 

I am trying to create a table that contains the current year to define a dynamic variable for data filtering in the AM Model.

I have used the following features:
 - Data Builder
 - New SQL View
 - SQLScript (Table Function)

I wrote the SQL statement as follows.

DECLARE T_RETURN TABLE (
    FISCPER NVARCHAR(7)
);

However, I am getting the following error.

sql syntax error: incorrect syntax near ",": line : line 2 col col 18 (at pos 60), Code: 257, SQL State: HY000

I didn't use a comma.
Any idea what could be causing this?

Any help would be welcome.

We have the 'Allow employees to enter introductory text' enabled on our people profile configuration. However, an inactive employee, before leaving has left an unsuitable comment on her profile and we need to try and remove this, without disabling the current configuration.

Does anyone know how we can do this?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP FSM+SAP S/4 Public cloud - integration equipment...
entry.content:
Hi,

By standard there is no way to send the counter reading from fsm to s4, however you can use Smartform capability of fsm to record such data and pass on to s4. thanks

entry.title: Re: Deficit of SL Unrestricted-use Message No. M7021
entry.content: Is the erroneous SMQ2 entry for the inbound delivery that you have posted or for some other outbound delivery?

entry.title: Missing functionality in SAP note : 955860 - Invoice correction process with return movement (PL&HU)
entry.content:
Hello, 

The SAP Note 955860 cannot be applied in the public cloud environment, as it requires the creation of a new sales document type (RERK), which is not supported.

We are implementing a Public Cloud solution for a company operating in Poland, and therefore this legal requirement must be addressed. Could you provide alternative solutions or recommendations ? 

Thank you in advance,

Regards, 

Eva

entry.title: How to add second contact and change the primary email to receiving notifications - SAP for Me cases
entry.content:
If I need to add a second contact person and change the primary email to receiving notifications, how can this be done? Also, is there any cost associated with making these changes? If there are any charges, please specify which parts incur a fee.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add second contact and change the primary emai...
entry.content:
The following KBA contains the guidelines about adding and removing case contacts:

2213199 How to add/remove additional contacts in an existing case - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One field mapping with FSM Cloud

entry.title: Authorization Error Setting Up SOAP Connection from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm currently setting up an integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Integration Suite (CPI) using a SOAP 1.X Sender Adapter. My goal is to send payment data from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to Integration Suite (Scope item 2YM), but I'm encountering a 401 Unauthorized error.

Setup:

  1. Communication User Setup:

    • Created a technical communication user in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  2. Communication System Setup:

    • Created a communication system in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
    • Entered what I believe is the correct host name (e.g. iFlow generated endpoint URL excluding Path).

  3. Communication Arrangement Setup:

    • Created a communication arrangement using the scenario SAP_COM_0256 (Bank Connectivity API).
    • Selected the correct communication system and technical user.

  4. Testing the Integration:

    • I'm testing the SOAP request using SOAPUI with Basic Authentication using the technical communication user's credentials. (This is also set up in Security Material in CPI).

Questions:

  1. How can I confirm that the communication user has the correct roles and authorizations for this setup?
  2. Is there any additional configuration needed in the iFlow on SAP Integration Suite to accept Basic Authentication?
  3. Could the 401 error be caused by an incorrect host name in the communication system setup?
  4. Would it be better to use Client Certificate Authentication, and if so, how should I set it up?
  5. Is the "SAP Process Integration Runtime" service mandatory for this setup, or can I establish the connection without it?

Any insights or guidance on resolving this 401 Unauthorized error would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025, featuring 50 exciting new projects aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration with our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • BTP AI based Data Anonymization Service
  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Multi-Region Desaster Recovery
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Provisioning, Integration with Joule 
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Concur

  • SAP Concur - AI Managed Expense Reports
  • SAP Concur - Dynamic Card Management – Virtual Credit Cards
  • SAP Concur Expense - Web Services for Financial Integration
  • SAP Concur Mobile app and the sign-in user experience
  • SAP Concur Solutions – All About E-Receipts
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Customer Managed Keys for granular control over data
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Sponsored Guest Feature
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Web Notifications Center and Preferences

SAP Customer Experience

  • Research project into a potential loyalty cloud solution
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

Data & Analytics

  • Redesigning the Dashboard Experience
  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

SAP Globalization

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report
  • ISO 21378 Audit Data Collection Standard
  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation
  • Sales and Use Tax Reporting in United States - User Research
  • Sales and Use Tax payments in United States

Lifecycle Management

  • DevOps with ABAP

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for External Workforce
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Hire to Retire Cloud
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Source-to-Pay

Others

  • Research into AI-Assisted User Learning Rollout for Adoption
  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis
  • The AI-Driven Future of New Product Introduction (NPI)

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Schedule Publication API's: Getting Started Guide

thank you for you detailed explanation.

kr, Michaela

entry.title: Re: List of S4HANA Credit Management Reports, Tables & Some issues we faced in our recent S4HANA Imp
entry.content:
Hi abprad,

Thank you very much for a nice blog full of helpful information.

I am just wondering... what about transaction UKM_MASS_RECON?
Can it be used for a check for differences between SD/FI and Credit Exposure?
For example, is it ok / recommended to schedule it on a regular basis to make sure all data is consistent?
Also, can it be used for FSCM credit management in S/4HANA (a single system) without any other systems or BW? Documentation of the report or SAP notes say it can be used to compare multiple systems and needs setup RSA5...

Best regards.
meow

entry.title: Re: BDC for ME01
entry.content: Check format of your data, batch input use dynpro conversion, so convert external to internal format where normally BAPI and IDOC will not and expect a correct format (check for conversion exit associated with domain MATNR and use FM associated).

entry.title: Re: Document number for CATS transfer posting to CO/PS
entry.content: Option is available for having separate CO document for individual employee, but haven't seen option for creating separate document for record dates.

entry.title: SAP For Me: display companies name in the list "open cases" when using the filter "Customer"
entry.content:
Hi all,

my S-User is assigned to two companies but I can only see the name of one company when using the filter "Customer" in the open cases list. The other company is only listed with the number. I'm planning to add several other companies to my S-User and this might get confusing when only the customer numbers show up. Is there a way to display the names of all companies that are assigned to my S-User?

entry.title: SAP FSM Checklist Instance Elément translations
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have several business rules that rely on the selected value of a dropdown field in Smartforms.

These rules are triggered based on specific values. For example, if the selected value is "Yes", the rule executes successfully.

However, since our Smartforms support multiple languages, users selecting "Oui" (French translation of "Yes") prevent the business rule from executing correctly.

Is there a way to retrieve the selected value in the dropdown in the default language ?

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Manually Editing the Amount in Settlement Document| S...
entry.content: Thanks for your response. Is there a way that we stop the Settlement from releasing into accounting and then edit the amount inside of the settlement document? Or the only way is to edit the Condition Contract itself?

entry.title: Is SAP Cloud ALM included in my Current Licenses? Does SAP Cloud ALM have fees?
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are in the process of initializing the Cloud ALM setup for our project. Before proceeding further, we would like to clarify the following:

  1. Is there any separate license cost for Cloud ALM, or is it covered under the current license?
  2. Are there any utilization limits based on consumption?
  3. How many users can access the Cloud ALM?
  4. Is a separate tenant required for each system landscape (Dev, Quality, and Prod)?
  5. To use SAP Signavio, which URL should be used, and what are the details regarding their tenant and license?

Looking forward for your reply.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Cloud ALM included in my Current Licenses? Doe...
entry.content:
Questions 1 and 2 are both addressed by KBA 3499485. In short, if your Customer number meets the requirements, you are entitled to one free SAP Cloud ALM tenant with an 8GB memory allocation. That memory is consumed when you use your SAP Cloud ALM to report on Metric data, etc. If you want to monitor your memory consumption, KBAs 3531392 and 3432729 will be useful.

Question 3 is addressed by KBA 3207614. In short, there is no user limit. 

The answer to question 4 is dependent on what you hope to use SAP Cloud ALM for, and how you wish to divide you landscape. Generally speaking, a single SAP Cloud ALM tenant can be easily used for your entire landscape. So multiple SAP Cloud ALM tenants are not required.

However, if you wish to do so you can purchase additional SAP Cloud ALM tenants ("extensions") and use them for whatever purpose you see fit (e.g. sub-dividing your landscape between locations, organizations, etc.). But a single tenant is usually sufficient to start with. If you later decide that you wish to use another tenant, you can purchase the extension when you need it. It is better to experiment and identify your use cases via the free tenant before making any additional purchases. 

Question 5 is addressed by KBAs 3473187 and 3549337.

entry.title: Re: Tax Line items are showing seperately as per exp in FB60
entry.content: Hi, I have the same problem. How was it solved? Thank you

entry.title: Re: Missing functionality in SAP note : 955860 - Invoice...
entry.content:
Hello @Eva11 

The note 955860 - PL and HU: Invoice correction process with returns movement references note 943783 - Intrastat Poland and Hungary: Processing invoice corrections, which is meant for SAP_APPL software component i.e. ECC:

I don't think you can implement either of the notes in S/4HANA Public Cloud. 

Have you confirmed with SAP that S/4HANA Public Cloud support Polish legal requirements before embarking on this risky journey?

This is what SAP Help says on Intrastat Declaration for Poland in S/4HANA Public Cloud:

That's not much, is it?

Also check the note 3537575 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2502, Local Versions: Release Restriction Note

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 - Weiterführung nach Testphase & ABAP-Entwicklung
entry.content: can anyone explain to us what the right way is ? What is the right licence ? So that we can continue with the SAP Cloud Appliance? And what the error is related to?

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
Hello @AndresCarter 

The idea is when the manager give the award, he will be able to see the defined guidelines 2%-10% of the Annual Base Salary per employee. The guideline will not have more criteria since will be configured per each program.

Many thanks!

Ana Lousada

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Launchpad: New Fast and Easy Option to Adapt SAP-delivered Launchpad Content (for On
entry.content:
Hello @ruth_groene ,

We are in 2021 release and currently, we referencing the SAP Standard Catalog content directly to our custom end user Business catalog.

We do not have something like a Custom technical catalog layer which we use for this reference. So will there be major issue during upgrade time with this approach?

entry.title: Re: QueryFailedError - Since Build Apps change 25th Feb
entry.content:
I am trying to find out more, and will post if I do.

When you say you created additional UUID, can you explain your use case? When you say UUID, I assume you mean 128-bit unique IDs. Where they UUIDs related to some other system, or created by you randomly for this DB.

And when you say sorting, I assume you mean the "Ordering" inside the "Get record collection" flow function?

And can you just show an example of what changed in terms of the casing?

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Bangalore, India)

entry.title: You need an MSI Installer for the SAP Business By Design Outlook Addin
entry.content:
You would like to have an MSI Installer rather then an exe Installer for the SAP Business ByDesign outlook Addin.

MSI Installer are not needed anymore as the Outlook Addin has the SAP Installer which includes the commands like /silent.

 

For more information please look here please, https://help.sap.com/sapsetup

 

 

alternatively if the Addin is already installed use NwSapSetup.exe /? to open the help instruction.

 

Our Plan is to move also the Excel Addin to the new installer.

Until that's done, for Excel we still provide an MSI Installer

entry.title: Re: QueryFailedError - Since Build Apps change 25th Feb
entry.content: I just created a new VCF entity and used in inside a new project. I was able to sort by my own UUID field. Could it be if I had an older database I can't now sort by such a field but a new database is OK?

entry.title: Search for the Root Cause of Inconsistency in /SCWM/AQUA
entry.content:
Introduction

During a Go-Live support phase, we encountered an inconsistency between physical and available stocks. While this issue is relatively rare—occurring perhaps once a day—it was still significant enough to investigate. Fixing it is usually straightforward with reports like /SCWM/CHM_LOG or /SCWM/CHM_PRF, but I wanted to understand the root cause. Unlike simple UI customization tasks, this case involved a deeper analysis, which made it particularly interesting for me.

Analyze

I decided to analyze the standard processes first. I waited until a new inconsistency appeared and started to check all related warehouse tasks for the Handling Unit and Product. As everyone knows, there should be no quant changes without a warehouse task. Finally, I was able to narrow down the time interval and analyze the application logs for this period. I found a strange error in the log, which I'll explain below.

The strange part was that the warehouse task was confirmed even though an error message was thrown in the log. Additionally, there were strange warehouse tasks confirmed with Product A when the Handling Unit had Product B. Luckily, this message was easy to find (Include /SCWM/LL03BFAF, form vquit_set_qty).

This form is called from the /SCWM/LL03BF0M form "QUITTIERUNG_PRUEFEN," which can be translated to "confirmation check." The vquit_set_qty is only called if cs_conf-vquit is not empty (I will return to this flag later). What is really interesting about this error message is the error handling. I show some other errors from these forms:

I asked Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT to collect the statistics, unfortunately both failed with this task, so no nice statistic here, and you have to trust me or recheck 🙂 all messages (or almost all messages) lead to:

 

PERFORM undo_conf USING <conf> ct_tap.
DELETE ct_conf.

 

But not the

 

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE e224 WITH cs_conf-vlenr INTO gv_msgtext.
    CALL METHOD go_prot->add_message( ip_row = cs_conf-seqno ).
    EXIT.
  ENDIF.

 

So, long story short, this error message is not throwing confirmed warehouse tasks from the confirmation table.

Thoughts regarding the VQUIT Flag

And regarding the VQUIT (Confirmation in Bulk Storage Area: Removal of Complete HU) flag, where use shows that in standard it could be filled only from the IDOC ( /SCWM/IDOC_INPUT_WMTOCO )

Confirmation in Bulk Storage Area: Removal of Complete HU

Definition
Means that the entire handling unit (HU) is removed without remaining quantities or difference quantities.

One can imagine that SAP has no other option, since the HU probably was physically moved away and the WT must be confirmed accordingly, but in this case it would be nice to have corresponding AQUA update in my opinion.

Conclusion & Solution

The case was solved by identifying that the combination of using the VQUIT flag with a lack of validation in the app led to the inconsistency. One can learn that it is better not to use flags that are not used in standard processes.
Since it was unclear how exactly data validation was bypassed by the user, and considering the difficulty of changing all the places where VQUIT could be set, we decided to implement a simple fix. We changed all calls of the /SCWM/TO_CONFIRM to set the IV_COMMIT_WORK to abap_false and handled the commit ourselves, performing a rollback in case of error messages in the ET_BAPIRET. This adjustment helped, and no more inconsistencies were identified since then.

entry.title: Request: SAP CodeJam(s) in Hamburg, Germany on Friday Sept 26, 2025
entry.content:
Hello Dev Advocates team, 

@Mark_Teichmann @hgoerke and I are planning to host an SAP Inside Track in Hamburg, Germany on Saturday, Sept 27, 2025. We would like to combine this with one or ideally two CodeJams on Friday, Sept 26.

  • Location:
    • SAP Office - Hamburg, Germany
  • Topics in which we are mostly interested:
    • 1: Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
    • 2: ABAP Cloud & ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
    • Alternative for 1: Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub
    • Alternative for 2: Connecting Systems and Services Using SAP Integration Suite
  • Time frame:
    • Friday, Sept 26, 2025 (date fixed because of the SIT event the next day.)

Please reach out to me for questions and planning purposes. Looking forward to hearing from you.

entry.title: Re: Service Call Scheduling via SDK
entry.content:
Hello,

This is a child object of the ServiceCall. You work with it the same way that your are used to with the DocumentLines object. Here is a sample code:

The code is using the DI API, but I suggest you to use Service Layer. Please refer to the ServiceCalls Entity in Service Layer API Reference documentation.

Best regards

I then added S_SERVICE *. When I load up the Launchpad all looks good, the catalog are showing in the finder but when I click on the apps nothing shows, the screen stays blank. Any ideas what I'm missing here?

entry.content:
Hello,

Your table function is incomplete and missing the necessary "RETURN SELECT ... FROM Table" statement, which is why you're encountering that error. Please complete the function and retest it. Please look at the below example to get an idea.

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
Hi Ana,

Apologies for the two comments, my keyboard was acting up 🙂
So, you could have the guidelines set with a value which you want to be a percentage. 
For example, below i have 2 for Mentoring and Team Player.

Within the business rules on the spotAward object we create the following.

We create a variable to get the salary of the employee, have the rule set to always true or for a selected program only, and use the Then to set the award amount to be salary*guideline.
When I tested this on an employee who's salary is 413,000.000 I got 2% correctly.

Hope this helps,
Andres

entry.title: Re: API for purchase order to insert invoicing plan details
entry.content:
Hi,
we are trying to create an invoicing plan via API for a purchase order. We are using the following API:
SAP Business Accelerator Hub
Sending out the following JSON:
{
"InvoicingPlan": "51",
"InvoicingPlanType": "10",
"InvoicingPlanStartDate": "2024-12-01",
"InvoicingPlanEndDate": "2025-02-26",
"InvoicingPlanNextInvcDateRule": "51",
"PurchasingOrganization": "2310",
"PurchasingGroup": "COR",
"PurchaseOrderType": "NB",
"Plant": "2310"
}

Call ends with positive result, but nothing happen on S4H Public.

Any suggestion?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Pricing Routine creation
entry.content: Will this BADI be used only in the Public Cloud, or also in the Private Cloud? In the Extensibility Registry, it states that the BADI is available for both Cloud and On-Premise. However, there seems to be no option to configure the BADI in either the On-Premise or Private Cloud environments

entry.title: Streamlining Dangerous Goods Compliance with SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Navigating the complex world of dangerous goods compliance just got easier with the latest update from SAP. On-Premise and Private Cloud customers can now receive new and updated dangerous goods compliance requirements and compliance requirement versions (CRV). This update ensures that the most current compliance requirement versions are at your fingertips, ready to be integrated into your system with minimal effort. Our Regulatory Content Service is available with SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 03 onward.

What’s New?

The new content delivery method allows for continuous updates of dangerous goods regulations embedded directly into SAP S/4HANA. This means that as soon as the system is connected to the content service, the latest compliance information is available for use, streamlining the process significantly compared to the previous method.

Activation Still Required

However, using the Activate Compliance Requirements – Dangerous Goods app, you still need to review and activate the new CRV. Otherwise, the new version will be in the system, but not in use. For more information on CRV activation, see the SAP Help Portal.

Future Developments

Stay tuned for updates found in our Roadmap, as we continue to enhance our services to better serve your needs.

We’re thrilled to offer this improved content delivery system, making dangerous goods compliance more efficient and manageable. For Regulatory Content Service setup information, refer to the relevant SAP Notes for public cloud, private cloud, and on-premise configurations. Setup information for the continuous delivery of dangerous goods regulations into SAP S/4HANA for product compliance can be found here.

Stay compliant, stay efficient, and let SAP S/4HANA guide you through the complexities of dangerous goods regulations with ease.

entry.title: SAP Joule use cases in Treasury
entry.content: I'd like to learn about any useful business use cases that leverage Joule to optimise Treasury operations.

entry.title: Re: BTP CPI. get Security Material in groovyscript problem
entry.content:
Hi, 
I tried the same, but as I want to have user and password secured, I did not want to switch to Secure Parameter. 
With this script I am able to read out also User Credentials.

I like to add Debug infos....not necessary, but nice to have.  

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory
import com.sap.it.api.securestore.SecureStoreService

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def map = message.getProperties()
    def debugCounter = map.keySet().findAll { it.startsWith("Debug_") }.size() + 1

    try {
        // Retrieve SecureStoreService instance (with null as the second argument)
        SecureStoreService secureStoreService = ITApiFactory.getService(SecureStoreService.class, null)
        if (secureStoreService == null) {
            message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Error: SecureStoreService could not be instantiated.")
            return message
        }

        // Access stored user credentials
        def credential = secureStoreService.getUserCredential("Name_of_Credential")
        if (credential == null) {
            message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Error: No credentials found for Credentials.")
            return message
        }

        // Extract username and password
        String username = credential.getUsername()
        String password = new String(credential.getPassword()) // Convert char array to String

        // Store debugging information
        message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Successfully retrieved credentials from Secure Store.")
        message.setProperty("Username", username)
        message.setProperty("Password", password) 

    } catch (Exception e) {
        message.setProperty("Debug_${debugCounter++}", "Exception while accessing Secure Store: " + e.getMessage())
    }

    return message
}

entry.title: Re: see 0F document type data in reconciliation balance u...
entry.content: Hi HengyeZhu, please, we have now updated Group Reporting version from 1909 to 2023. we're using cds ZC_JOURNAL_ENTRIES_05 copy of ICA_CONSJOURNALENTRIES_5. We're facing an error related to FYV setting. Even in this version, we're not able to insert in the mandatory filter field the parameter FYV K4 (already present in Version and Consolidation unit). Could you kindly help us to understand what we are missing on our configurations? Do you have any hints? Thank you in advance for any other suggestion!

entry.title: Re: Import data from BW Bex query to SAC system
entry.content: Going to edit mode of master data import job - will not allow to modify the existing mapping nor allow to create new job from that edit query screen. So how do we create copy of existing master data import job, Am I missing anything ?
